summary: Tested new separate sprint/main prediction endpoints and admin members feature. Found and fixed MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in sprint/main endpoints. All 16 backend tests pass. Frontend verification shows Sprint/Course tabs, double countdown, and admin Members tab working correctly.

fixed.issue: MongoDB ObjectId not serializable when returning existing prediction - caused 500 error
fixed.fix: Added _id exclusion: existing_clean = {k: v for k, v in existing.items() if k != '_id'}

success_rate.backend: 100% (16 passed, 3 skipped - skipped due to auth dependency chain)

seed_data_creation: Created multiple test users during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: The new /api/predictions/sprint and /api/predictions/main endpoints are now working. These endpoints update the same prediction document independently. Miami-2026 is a sprint weekend (is_sprint_weekend=true). Japan-2026 is a classic weekend. Sprint predictions close 15min before SQ1, main predictions close 15min before Q1.

tests: Accepts valid sprint predictions for sprint weekend
Works for both sprint and classic weekends
Both endpoints update same prediction document
Returns list of all users with email, predictions_count, leagues_count
Returns full member details (id, email, username, level, xp, stats, leagues, recent_predictions)
Sprint/Course tabs visible for sprint weekends (Miami)
Deadline text shows 'Clôture 15 min avant SQ1' for sprint
Step navigation (Pole, Top 10, Winner, Top 10, Bonus) visible
MEMBRES tab visible with 53 registered members
Each member shows username, email, level, prediction count
REACTION and BATAK game tabs visible
Start button and game controls available

bug_fixed_during_testing.description: MongoDB ObjectId serialization error in prediction update endpoints
bug_fixed_during_testing.root_cause: When updating existing predictions, the code returned {**existing, **new_data} but 'existing' contained the MongoDB '_id' field which is not JSON serializable
bug_fixed_during_testing.fix_applied: Added exclusion of '_id' field before merging: existing_clean = {k: v for k, v in existing.items() if k != '_id'}
